page: Vultr vhf-6c-24gb is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the High Frequency Compute family. It features 6 shared Intel Core Skylake vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 24.0 GB of system memory, which yields a 4.0 GB per core ratio. The hardware configuration includes a bundled 448 GB NVMe SSD for local storage and one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities across standard compute tasks. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests score 1670.29 and 9438.87 respectively, placing them in the average tier. Single-threaded gzip compression is a notable strength at 33.56 GB/sec. However, memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is weak to poor, with write speeds dropping to 13.1 GB/sec. Similarly, database operations and static web serving throughput fall into the weak category. Large-model LLM inference is poor, though small-model text generation achieves an average of 108.96 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a high-density allocation of NVMe storage and memory relative to its shared CPU resource class. Because the CPU allocation is shared, it is best suited for workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated CPU performance. Recommended workloads include general-purpose web serving, small-scale database operations, caching layers via Redis, which performs at an average of 7.25 million operations per second, and lightweight file compression tasks.
description: The Vultr vhf-6c-24gb is an x86_64 virtual server from the High Frequency Compute family, configured with 6 shared Intel Core Skylake vCPUs clocked at 2.0 GHz, 24.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 448 GB NVMe SSD. It delivers average performance across standard compute benchmarks, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 9438.87 and strong single-threaded gzip compression at 33.56 GB/sec. However, it exhibits limitations in large-block memory bandwidth and database operations. Given its shared CPU architecture and balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server provides a cost-effective option for workloads such as general-purpose web serving, Redis caching, and moderate file processing where dedicated CPU cores are not strictly required.
og_description: Vultr vhf-6c-24gb is an x86_64 High Frequency Compute server featuring 6 shared Intel vCPUs, 24 GB RAM, and a 448 GB NVMe SSD, delivering balanced general-purpose performance for web serving and caching.
meta_description: Vultr vhf-6c-24gb is an x86_64 server with 6 shared Intel vCPUs, 24 GB RAM, and 448 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced shared-CPU server featuring fast NVMe storage and moderate memory capacity for general-purpose workloads and caching.
bullet_points: 6 shared Intel Core Skylake vCPUs at 2.0 GHz
24.0 GB system memory with a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Bundled 448 GB NVMe SSD local storage
Suitable for Redis caching and general-purpose web serving
